summary: This page introduces time-invariant and time-varying parameter systems and explains the time-invariance property: delaying the input by T seconds delays the output by the same amount under identical initial conditions. It notes that systems governed by linear differential equations with constant coefficients are linear time-invariant (LTI), while time-dependent coefficients produce linear time-varying systems, illustrated by a carbon microphone example and an exercise involving y(t) = (sin t) f(t - 2).
